description_long: This object is a weathered, single-propeller military-style airplane with a dark, metallic fuselage showing significant rust and paint chipping. The wings are straight and low-mounted, each bearing roundel insignias reminiscent of World War II-era aircraft. The cockpit is enclosed with a blue-tinted canopy, positioned just behind the propeller and ahead of the wing roots. The tail features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, also marked with roundels. The landing gear is partially visible and appears to be fixed, with the main gear under the wings and a tailwheel at the rear. The overall appearance suggests a vintage, battle-worn aircraft, possibly used as a static display or urban art installation.
description: A weathered, dark metallic single-propeller airplane with roundel insignias, rust patches, and an enclosed blue-tinted cockpit. The aircraft features a conventional tail and fixed landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the propeller, cockpit canopy, and significant rust and paint chipping on the fuselage.
description_view_1: Rear oblique view highlighting the tail section, roundel insignias on the wings and tail, and the fixed landing gear.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, cockpit placement, and the distribution of rust and insignias.
description_view_3: Front oblique view focusing on the propeller, nose, and the forward section of the fuselage.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a blue-tinted canopy.
